title: Packed-width promotion boundaries are not directly covered
evidence: The packed tests exercise corpus warmup and ranking parity, but they do not construct exact boundary cases such as field tf=255 vs 256, field tf=65535 vs 65536, numeric doc id=65535 vs 65536, termId=65535 vs 65536, or posting indexes 127/128 with assertions on parity. The 10,245-document corpus cannot promote doc ids past Uint16, and its generated body terms are spread across 256 distinct terms per document, so field-tf promotion is not forced by this test.
rationale: The implementation currently uses correct `>` thresholds and widens existing allocated chunks, but a future `>=` regression or missed chunk-width regression could silently truncate scores without a focused boundary test.
